description: True/false evaluation for the improve-bot skill, anchored on the structured morning-report markdown (Phase 5) plus the run-start header, fixed-string rules, and conditional flavor behavior.

evals.statement: The morning report contains a run-duration line AND a stop-reason line (e.g. 'wall-clock budget exhausted', '3 consecutive failed attempts', 'watchdog trip').
The morning report reports baseline-to-final metric deltas for every measure selected in Phase 1 (not just one measure when multiple were configured).
The morning report includes an attempt table whose columns are exactly slug, issue #, branch, result, and SHA.
Every result value in the attempt table is one of the fixed forms 'merged', 'failed', or 'rolled-back' — no free-form result wording.

The morning report includes a 'Suggested next action' entry phrased as a concrete action (e.g. 'soak-N with same config', 'fix issue #X and retry', 'metric Y regressed, investigate'), not a vague 'continue improving'.
The morning report includes links to every GitHub issue opened during the run.
The report contains a 'Tag cheatsheet' section.
The tag cheatsheet's list-everything command is exactly 'git tag -l "improve-bot/run/$RUN_TS/*"' with the literal glob suffix '/*'.
The tag cheatsheet's NUKE-whole-run command resets hard to the baseline tag 'improve-bot/run/$RUN_TS/baseline' (not to a remote ref and not to master).
The tag cheatsheet includes the see-what-landed command 'git log "improve-bot/run/$RUN_TS/baseline..master" --oneline'.
The run-start header records BOTH the SKILL_START timestamp (when /improve-bot was invoked) AND the PHASE4_T0 timestamp (when Phase 4 iteration begins).
The run-start header lists flavor, the selected measures, stop conditions, data state, and the on/off state of the --demo and --self-improve-code flags.
When the leak check fired, the run-start header records INTERACTIVE_ELAPSED and which option (a/b/c) was chosen.
If the flavor is demo, the run-start header's baseline-tag field reads 'n/a (demo)' rather than naming a created tag.
If the flavor is demo, the output creates and pushes NO git tag (no 'improve-bot/run/.../baseline' tag is created); RUN_TS exists only as a local logging var.
When the classified flavor is training (a metric axis or empty suggestion with no flags), the output enables the daemon (launches with --daemon) and performs no source-code commits.
If --demo and --self-improve-code are both present, the skill stops and asks which the user meant rather than proceeding with a flavor.
When INTERACTIVE_ELAPSED exceeds 30 min at Phase 3 entry, the output STOPS and presents exactly three options (a) Reset / (b) Absorb / (c) Stop rather than silently entering Phase 4.
The output never issues a plain 'git push --force' (force-push appears only as the '--force-with-lease' automatic-rollback path in Phase 4).
The output contains no 'git reset --hard' targeting a remote ref (e.g. origin/master); reset-hard appears only against a local tag the skill itself created this run.
The output never deletes a 'baseline', 'merged', or 'final' tag.
No git command in the output uses '--no-verify' to bypass hooks.
In a hybrid run, no source-code merge or push to master occurs unless --self-improve-code is set (without the flag, hybrid degrades to training-only with no code touched).
The Phase 4 wall-clock budget checks measure elapsed time from PHASE4_T0, not from SKILL_START or the baseline tag timestamp.
The output is a single coherent run-plan/report that names a flavor and at least one performance measure (default 'win rate vs difficulty 1' when unspecified).

When at least one automatic rollback fired during the run, the morning report's rollback log names the specific prior tag the run reset HEAD to (the '/<slug>/merged' or '/baseline' tag used as the rollback target), not merely a free-text note that 'a rollback occurred'.
evals.source: SKILL.md, lines 280-285 (PREV target) + line 313 ('Rollback log if any fired'); code-quality.md (traceability)

evals.statement: When the run stopped because of 3 consecutive failed attempts (zero merged work), the morning report states the no-progress outcome explicitly and reports the baseline-to-final metric delta as zero/unchanged (or regressed) WITHOUT claiming any improvement; failures are surfaced, not hidden behind an empty or omitted attempt table.
evals.source: SKILL.md, lines 292 (3 consecutive failed -> stop) + 322-323 (deltas + attempt table); code-quality.md silent-exception-fallthrough / 'failures not hidden'

evals.statement: For a demo run, the report includes an explicit data/-integrity verdict line stating the stop-time diff of data/ against the data.demo-snapshot was clean (no mutation, demo contract upheld) — not merely an implicit 'demo finished'.
evals.source: SKILL.md, line 198 + lines 64, 112 (snapshot is the contract; any diff flagged loudly)

evals.statement: For a demo run, the report states the outcome of the §3.5 synthetic alert pre-flight (the alerts-pipeline verification), since that verification is the primary deliverable of demo flavor.

When the Phase 3 wall-clock leak check fired and the operator chose (a) Reset, the run-start header records that the unsupervised daemon work was archived as a 'bonus observation' (not silently discarded) AND that PHASE4_T0 was re-anchored to the post-reset start time used for the Phase 4 budget.
evals.source: SKILL.md, lines 161, 174 (option a archives + re-anchors); scenario_3 context

evals.statement: The morning report references posting a short summary to the umbrella GitHub issue (the run's 'self-improve-run'-labeled issue) so the report is phone-readable — not only the local run-log writeup.

When a rollback fired, the attempt-table row for the rolled-back attempt carries the fixed result token 'rolled-back' (and the corresponding GitHub issue is reported as commented with the regression, not silently closed as a clean success).
